app.medical_disclaimer: AINA is an information tool. Its content does not replace professional medical advice.

onboarding.birth_date_subtitle: And is it a boy or a girl?
onboarding.measurements_subtitle: We need them to follow growth.
onboarding.country_subtitle: The vaccination schedule will match the country's EPI.
onboarding.vaccines_subtitle: Tick the vaccines {{name}} has already received for a tailored follow-up.

growth_interpretation.normal_message: {{name}}'s {{metric}} follows a nice curve in the average range for their age. Keep it up!
growth_interpretation.watch_message: {{name}} is slightly above or below average but still within normal limits. We'll keep an eye on it at the next visit.
growth_interpretation.alert_low_message: The {{metric}} is below the 3rd percentile. Nothing alarming in itself, but a pediatrician's opinion will help evaluate the overall curve.
growth_interpretation.alert_high_message: The {{metric}} is above the 97th percentile. A visit with your pediatrician will help put it in context.
growth_interpretation.curve_break_message: {{name}}'s curve has crossed several bands between measurements. It's wise to mention this to the pediatrician soon.

red_flag_checker.header_subtitle: Describe what you observe, no diagnosis — just guidance.
red_flag_checker.observation_placeholder: Ex. Fever of 38.5 since this morning, baby refuses bottle, unusual crying…
red_flag_checker.observation_hint: The more precise (duration, temperature, behavior), the better the AI can help.
red_flag_checker.disclaimer: ⚕️ This information does not replace your pediatrician's advice.

chat.welcome: Hello, mum of {{name}} 👋 I'm AINA AI, the app's assistant. Ask me your questions about baby's health, sleep, feeding or development.
chat.welcome_disclaimer: ⚕️ I don't replace your pediatrician — if in doubt, consult.
chat.limit_reached: Limit of 10 questions reached — start a new conversation or come back later.
chat.suggestion_cow_milk: How do I introduce cow's milk?
chat.suggestion_sleep: My baby sleeps badly at night, any advice?
chat.suggestion_fever: When should I consult for a fever?

ai_recipe.ingredients_placeholder: millet, mango, milk, sweet potato, carrot…
ai_recipe.ingredients_hint: Separated by commas — or leave empty for a free suggestion.
ai_recipe.recipe_saved_desc: Find it in My AI recipes.
ai_recipe.milk_only_body: WHO recommends exclusive breastfeeding (or infant formula) until 6 months. No solid food is needed before this age.
ai_recipe.milk_only_months: {{name}} is {{months}} months old — {{remaining}} more month(s) before food introduction.
ai_recipe.milk_only_preview: See recipes to prepare for food introduction
ai_recipe.preview_mode_banner: Preview mode — recipes for when baby turns 6 months

profile_form.confirm_delete_body: All data for {{name}} will be permanently deleted.

dashboard.care_kit_hint: Dose calculator, fever guide, stool analysis

vaccines.disclaimer: This information is indicative. Your pediatrician remains the reference for any personalised question.
vaccines.how_body_defense_html: Your baby's body has a natural defense army: its immune system. But that army needs to learn how to recognize the enemies.
vaccines.how_vaccine_role_html: The vaccine shows the body a harmless version of the germ. When the real germ shows up, the defenses are already ready.
vaccines.how_herd_immunity_html: Vaccinating baby also means protecting others: fragile people, future big sisters and brothers, pregnant women, seniors.
vaccines.how_side_effects_note: Side effects (mild fever, redness) are temporary and minor. Always rare compared with the diseases they prevent.

growth.no_data: No measurement recorded yet. Add one to see interpretation.

teeth.tap_hint: Tap a tooth to mark it as erupted

development.red_flags_intro: If at this age {{name}} shows one or more of these signs, talk to your pediatrician:
development.disclaimer: Every child grows at their own pace — these markers are indicative, only a professional can give a diagnosis.

health_report.no_milestones: No milestone available for this age.
health_report.footer: Record generated by AINA — Pediatric follow-up by Dr Helminah Randriamananoro

nutrition.title: Nutrition

foods.detail_conservation_note: Store homemade purées in the fridge up to 48h (airtight container) or 1 month in the freezer. Reheat only the portion to eat, never twice.

nutrition.no_favorites_hint: Tap the heart to save your favorite recipes.
nutrition.empty_shopping_hint: Plan your meals to generate the shopping list.
nutrition.age_guard_food_body: {{name}} is {{months}} month(s) old. WHO recommends exclusive breastfeeding or formula until 6 months. No solid food, no tea, no juice.
nutrition.age_guard_food_unlock: Recipes and food guide will be available at 6 months.
nutrition.age_guard_food_rule2: No tea, juice or sweetened water
nutrition.age_guard_food_rule3: Breast milk or formula only (WHO recommendation)
nutrition.age_guard_planner_body: {{name}} is {{months}} month(s) old. Meal planning and shopping list unlock at 4 months, when food introduction may begin with your pediatrician's guidance.
nutrition.hint_foods: 🥕 Food guide — what to introduce and when, by your baby's age

nutrition.hint_planner: 📅 Plan your weekly meals, day by day
nutrition.hint_shopping: 🛒 Your shopping list generated from the meal plan

journal.title: Journal
journal.empty_hint: Use the + button to add an event.

care.title: Care

dose.max_interval: Every 6h max ({{count}} doses/24h) · adjust with fever.
dose.ibuprofen_warning: Contraindicated before 3 months, with chickenpox or dehydration.

fever.baby_under_3m_body: Any fever ≥ 38°C is an emergency. Consult immediately.

care.disclaimer: This information is indicative. Always check the leaflet and consult a doctor if in doubt. Emergency: call 15 / 112.

bcg.why: Protects baby against a serious disease that mainly affects the lungs.
bcg.disease: Tuberculosis is caused by a germ that attacks the lungs (and sometimes other organs). It spreads by coughing.
bcg.risk: In infants, tuberculosis can cause severe forms (meningitis, extensive pulmonary).
bcg.side_effects: Small local reaction that may leave a scar on the arm, normal.

hepb.why: Prevents baby from catching a virus that damages the liver long term.
hepb.disease: Hepatitis B is a virus that settles in the liver. Can become chronic and cause cirrhosis or cancer.
hepb.risk: Can be transmitted from mother to baby at birth or within the family.
hepb.side_effects: Redness at injection site, mild fever possible.

polio.why: Prevents a disease that can paralyze for life.
polio.disease: Poliomyelitis is a virus that attacks the nervous system and can paralyze legs, arms, or breathing.
polio.risk: No cure — only vaccination prevents it. Still active in some countries.

penta.why: A single vaccine that protects against 5 serious diseases.
penta.disease: Diphtheria (choking), Tetanus (contractures), Pertussis (severe cough), Hepatitis B, Haemophilus (meningitis).
penta.risk: These diseases are all potentially fatal in infants.
penta.side_effects: Fever (paracetamol allowed), redness, baby a bit fussy 24-48h.

hexa.why: Protects against 6 diseases in a single injection.
hexa.disease: Diphtheria, Tetanus, Pertussis, Polio, Hib (meningitis), Hepatitis B.
hexa.risk: Without vaccine, these diseases can be serious or fatal.
hexa.side_effects: Fever, local redness, fussy baby 24-48h. Paracetamol if fever.

pneumo.why: Protects lungs and brain against a dangerous bacterium.
pneumo.disease: Pneumococcus causes ear infections, pneumonia and meningitis in young children.
pneumo.risk: Pneumococcal meningitis can leave neurological sequelae.

rota.why: Avoids very severe diarrhea that can dehydrate baby.
rota.disease: Rotavirus causes acute gastroenteritis in infants.
rota.risk: Rapid dehydration — main cause of hospitalization for gastroenteritis before age 2.
rota.side_effects: Oral vaccine, few side effects, sometimes slightly soft stools.

ror.why: Triple protection against 3 highly contagious diseases.
ror.disease: Measles (rash + high fever), Mumps (swollen glands), Rubella (dangerous if pregnant).
ror.risk: Measles remains one of the deadliest childhood diseases without vaccine.
ror.side_effects: Mild fever and rash 7-10 days after vaccination (normal reaction).

fievre-jaune.why: Protects against a mosquito-borne disease, serious in Africa.
fievre-jaune.disease: Yellow fever causes fever, jaundice and can affect liver and kidneys.
fievre-jaune.risk: Potentially fatal disease. Endemic in many African countries.

meningo-a.why: Protects against meningitis in the African "meningitis belt".
meningo-a.disease: Meningococcus A causes rapidly fatal meningitis.
meningo-a.risk: Seasonal epidemics in the Sahel, children are most affected.

meningo-c.disease: Meningococcal C infection: meningitis or generalized blood infection.
meningo-c.risk: Can kill in hours. The vaccine is very effective.

vita.why: Strengthens immunity and prevents vision problems.
vita.disease: Vitamin A deficiency causes night blindness and reduced immunity.
vita.risk: Common in some regions with limited food variety.
vita.side_effects: None at standard dose. Oral drops.

4.label: 4 months
signs: Doesn't follow a moving object with eyes

9.label: 9 months
signs: Can't sit up even with support
Doesn't react to sounds or their name

24.label: 24 months
signs: Shows no interest in other children

teeth_tips: 🧊 A cold teething ring soothes sore gums
👆 Gently massage gums with a clean finger
💊 Paracetamol if mild fever related to teething
🍌 Offer cold foods (cold compote, fresh banana)
🚫 Avoid numbing gels — not recommended before age 2

steps.title: Assess the situation
steps.desc: Is baby coughing? Can they cry or make sounds? If yes, encourage them to cough.

steps.title: Position
steps.desc: Place baby face down on your forearm, head lower than body, supporting the jaw.

steps.title: 5 back slaps
steps.desc: Give 5 firm slaps between the shoulder blades with the heel of your hand.

steps.title: 5 chest compressions
steps.desc: Turn baby on their back. Perform 5 compressions in the middle of the sternum with 2 fingers.

steps.title: Repeat or call help
steps.desc: Alternate slaps and compressions. If obstruction persists, call emergency immediately (15 / 112).

fever_advice.mild: Undress, hydrate often, monitor. Paracetamol not required if baby is comfortable.
fever_advice.moderate_template: Paracetamol {{doseMg}} mg every 6h. Cool the room (18-20°C).
fever_advice.high: Consult quickly, especially if baby < 3 months or fever > 48h.
emergency_signs: Repeated vomiting or total refusal to drink

stool_types.desc: Breast milk: golden-yellow stools, lumpy, mild odor.
Formula or solids: more formed, browner.
Occasionally normal. If persistent: too much lactose or digestive discomfort.
Possible liver issue. Consult a pediatrician quickly.
Consult quickly. May indicate allergy, fissure, or more.
May indicate digestive bleeding. Immediate consultation.

stool_note: 💧 Diarrhea = more than 3 very liquid stools per day. Risk of dehydration: hydrate frequently, watch the diaper, consult if no improvement in 24h.

storage_rules.title: Homemade purée
storage_rules.detail: Airtight container closed right after cooking

storage_rules.title: Infant formula bottle
storage_rules.detail: Prepare at the last moment, throw out the rest

storage_rules.title: Hard-boiled egg
storage_rules.detail: Must be fully cooked for baby

storage_golden_rules: Reheat only once, thoroughly (70°C min for meat/fish).
Throw away any leftover started by baby (saliva) after 1h.

hygiene_sections.title: Before cooking
items: Wash your hands with soap for 20 seconds
Clean worktop + utensils with hot soapy water
Wash fruits and vegetables with clean water (brush if thick skin)
Watch wooden utensils — prefer stainless steel/plastic

hygiene_sections.title: Bottle sterilization (0-4 months)
items: Fully disassemble the bottle (nipple, ring, cap)
Wash each piece with hot soapy water + rinse
Sterilize in boiling water 10 min OR steam sterilizer
Let air dry on a clean cloth
After 4 months: dishwasher at 65°C is enough

hygiene_sections.title: Prepare an infant formula bottle
items: Respect the dose: 1 level scoop per 30 ml of water
Pour water first then powder, never the reverse
Test temperature on wrist (warm, never hot)

hygiene_sections.title: Baby-friendly cooking
items: No salt, no sugar, no honey before 12 months
No strong spices (chili, pepper) before age 2

hygiene_temperature_note: Ideal temperature: baby's meal should be warm (about 37°C). Never hot, never cold before 12 months.

doctor.about_aina_body: AINA means "Life, Breath" in Malagasy. This app was born out of Dr Helminah's passion for pediatrics and her drive to make baby follow-up accessible to every mother, in Africa and around the world.

about.title: About Dr Helminah
about.subtitle: Pediatrician · Creator of AINA
about.bio: Originally from Madagascar, Helminah is completing her pediatrics training in Dakar. Every day she meets families searching for answers and finding none. A content creator and digital strategist for 4 years, passionate about AI — she put all that energy into AINA, so every parent can get a clear answer, in their language, at any hour.

intro.slide1_body: Your personal pediatric companion, available 24/7, in your language. Recipes, vaccines, growth tracking — everything for your baby's wellbeing.
intro.slide2_body: Enter the ingredients you have at home, and AINA generates a recipe adapted to your baby's exact age. Millet, cassava, mango — their food, not a textbook recipe.
intro.slide3_body: Ask any question, send a photo. AINA guides you without ever making a diagnosis. In Wolof, Malagasy, French or English.
